      self._cache_hits += 1\n94\t                age = time.time() - (expiry_timestamp - self.ttl_seconds)\n95\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache HIT for key {key[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n96\t                return intent_mapping\n97\t            else:\n98\t                # Remove expired entry\n99\t                del self._cache[key]\n100\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache EXPIRED for key {key[:8]}\")\n101\t\n102\t        # Cache miss or expired\n103\t        self._cache_misses += 1\n104\t        return None\n105\t\n106\t    def set(self, key: str, value: list) -> None:\n107\t        \"\"\"\n108\t        Store intent mapping with expiry timestamp.\n109\t\n110\t        Args:\n111\t            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n112\t            value: Intent mapping to cache (list[IntentClassification])\n113\t        \"\"\"\n114\t        # Prune cache if at capacity (evict oldest entry - first in dict)\n115\t        if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:\n116\t            oldest_key = next(iter(self._cache))\n117\t            del self._cache[oldest_key]\n118\t            logger.debug(f\"Cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n119\t\n120\t        # Store with expiry timestamp\n121\t        expiry_timestamp = time.time() + self.ttl_seconds\n122\t        self._cache[key] = (value, expiry_timestamp)\n123\t        logger.debug(f\"Cache STORED key {key[:8]} (cache size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n124\t\n125\t    def _cleanup_expired(self) -> int:\n126\t        \"\"\"\n127\t        Remove all expired entries from the cache.\n128\t\n129\t        Returns:\n130\t            Number of entries removed\n131\t        \"\"\"\n132\t        current_time = time.time()\n133\t        expired_keys = [\n134\t            key for key, (_, expiry) in self._cache.items()\n135\t            if expiry < current_time\n136\t        ]\n137\t\n138\t        for key in expired_keys:\n139\t            del self._cache[key]\n140\t\n141\t        if expired_keys:\n142\t            logger.debug(f\"Cache cleanup removed {len(expired_keys)} expired entries\")\n143\t\n144\t        return len(expired_keys)\n145\t\n146\t    def get_stats(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n147\t        \"\"\"\n148\t        Get cache statistics.\n149\t\n150\t        Returns:\n151\t            Dict with hits, misses, hit_rate, size\n152\t        \"\"\"\n153\t        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n154\t        hit_rate = (self._cache_hits / total_requests * 100) if total_requests > 0 else 0.0\n155\t\n156\t        return {\n157\t            \"hits\": self._cache_hits,\n158\t            \"misses\": self._cache_misses,\n159\t            \"hit_rate\": hit_rate,\n160\t            \"size\": len(self._cache),\n161\t        }\n162\t\n163\t    def clear(self) -> None:\n164\t        \"\"\"Clear the cache and reset statistics.\"\"\"\n165\t        self._cache.clear()\n166\t        self._cache_hits = 0\n167\t        self._cache_misses = 0\n168\t        logger.debug(\"Cache cleared\")\n169\t\n170\t    def should_log_stats(self) -> bool:\n171\t        \"\"\"\n172\t        Check if stats should be logged based on operation interval.\n173\t\n174\t        Returns:\n175\t            True if stats should be logged, False otherwise\n176\t        \"\"\"\n177\t        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n178\t        return total_requests > 0 and total_requests % self._stats_log_interval == 0\n179\t\n180\t\n181\t# Router error types for degraded-state handling\n182\tclass RouterError(Exception):\n183\t    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n184\t    pass\n185\t\n186\t\n187\tclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n188\t    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n189\t    pass\n190\t\n191\t\n192\tclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n193\t    \"\"\"Router LLM call quota exhausted.\"\"\"\n194\t    pass\n195\t\n196\t\n197\tclass RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n198\t    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n199\t    pass\n200\t\n201\t\n202\tclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n203\t    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n204\t\n205\t    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n206\t        self.parse_error = parse_error\n207\t        self.raw_output = raw_output\n208\t        self.retry_count = retry_count\n209\t        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n210\t\n211\t\n212\tclass IntentType(Enum):\n213\t    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n214\t    STATUS = \"status\"\n215\t    ACTION = \"action\"\n216\t    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n217\t    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n218\t    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n219\t    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n220\t    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n221\t    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n222\t    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n223\t    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n224\t\n225\t\n226\t@dataclass\n227\tclass IntentClassification:\n228\t    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n229\t    intent_type: IntentType\n230\t    project_slug: str | None = None\n231\t    confidence: float = 1.0\n232\t    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n233\t    reasoning: str = \"\"\n234\t    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n235\t    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n236\t\n237\t\n238\t@dataclass\n239\tclass RoutedIntent:\n240\t    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n241\t    intent_id: str\n242\t    classification: IntentClassification\n243\t    session_id: str\n244\t    utterance: str\n245\t    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n246\t    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n247\t    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n248\t    router_ms: int | None = None\n249\t\n250\t\n251\t# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n252\t# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n253\t# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\n254\tROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n255\t\n256\t# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n257\t_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n258\t    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n259\t    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n260\t    \"intent objects.\"\n261\t)\n262\t\n263\t\n264\tclass IntentRouter:\n265\t    \"\"\"\n266\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n267\t\n268\t    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n269\t    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n270\t\n271\t    Latency optimizations:\n272\t    - LRU cache for repeated utterances (5-minute TTL)\n273\t    - Reduced max_tokens (128 vs 256) for faster generation\n274\t    - Dedicated 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n275\t    \"\"\"\n276\t\n277\t    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None, cache_ttl: int = 300):\n278\t        self.store = store\n279\t        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n280\t        self._zai_client = None\n281\t        self._router_zai_client = None  # Dedicated client with 10s timeout\n282\t        self._reload_manager = None\n283\t        self._cache = IntentCache(ttl_seconds=cache_ttl)\n284\t\n285\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n286\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n287\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n288\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n289\t        return self._zai_client\n290\t\n291\t    async def _get_router_zai_client(self):\n292\t        \"\"\"Get or create dedicated ZAI client for router with optimized settings for low latency.\"\"\"\n293\t        if self._router_zai_client is None:\n294\t            from ..escalate.llm import get_router_zai_client\n295\t            # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\n296\t            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\n297\t            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n298\t        return self._router_zai_client\n299\t\n300\t    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n301\t        \"\"\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\"\"\"\n302\t        import hashlib\n303\t        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\n304\t\n305\t    def generate_cache_key(self, utterance: str, intent_type_context: str | None = None) -> str:\n306\t        \"\"\"\n307\t        Generate a SHA256 hash key for caching based on utterance and optional context.\n308\t\n309\t        Args:\n310\t            utterance: The user utterance to hash\n311\t            intent_type_context: Optional context string (e.g., intent type, session state)\n312\t\n313\t        Returns:\n314\t            SHA256 hex digest (32 characters) of the combined utterance and context\n315\t\n316\t        Examples:\n317\t            >>> generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", \"status\")\n318\t            'a1b2c3d4e5f6...'  # 32-character hex string\n319\t            >>> generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", None)  # Same as no context\n320\t            'differenthashvalue...'  # Different hash without context\n321\t        \"\"\"\n322\t        import hashlib\n323\t\n324\t        # Handle None/empty context gracefully - treat as empty string\n325\t        context = intent_type_context or \"\"\n326\t\n327\t        # Combine utterance and context with a delimiter to avoid collisions\n328\t        # e.g., \"test\" + \"status\" vs \"teststatus\" + \"\"\n329\t        combined = f\"{utterance}|{context}\"\n330\t\n331\t        # Generate SHA256 hash and return hex digest (32 characters)\n332\t        return hashlib.sha256(combined.encode()).hexdigest()\n333\t\n334\t    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n335\t        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n336\t        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n337\t        return self._cache.get(cache_key)\n338\t\n339\t    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n340\t        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n341\t        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n342\t        self._cache.set(cache_key, classifications)\n343\t\n344\t    def _log_cache_stats_if_needed(self) -> None:\n345\t        \"\"\"Log cache hit rate statistics periodically.\"\"\"\n346\t        if self._cache.should_log_stats():\n347\t            stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n348\t            logger.info(\n349\t                f\"Router cache statistics: \"\n350\t                f\"hits={stats['hits']} misses={stats['misses']} \"\n351\t                f\"hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.1f}% \"\n352\t                f\"total_requests={stats['hits'] + stats['misses']} \"\n353\t                f\"cache_size={stats['size']}\"\n354\t            )\n355\t\n356\t    def _clear_cache(self) -> None:\n357\t        \"\"\"Clear the router cache. Used primarily for testing and diagnostics.\"\"\"\n358\t        self._cache.clear()\n359\t\n360\t    async def _get_store(self):\n361\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n362\t        if self.store is None:\n363\t            self.store = get_store()\n364\t        return self.store\n365\t\n366\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n367\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n368\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n369\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n370\t        return self._reload_manager\n371\t\n372\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n373\t        \"\"\"\n374\t        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n375\t\n376\t        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n377\t        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n378\t        \"\"\"\n379\t        try:\n380\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n381\t        except Exception as e:\n382\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n383\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n384\t\n385\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n386\t        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n387\t\n388\t        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n389\t        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n390\t        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n391\t        \"\"\"\n392\t        return self._load_router_prompt()\n393\t\n394\t    async def classify_utterance(\n395\t        self,\n396\t        utterance: str,\n397\t        session_id: str,\n398\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n399\t    ) -> tuple[list[IntentClassification], dict]:\n400\t        \"\"\"\n401\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n402\t\n403\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n404\t\n405\t        Args:\n406\t            utterance: The user utterance\n407\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n408\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n409\t\n410\t        Returns:\n411\t            Tuple of (classifications list, timing_breakdown dict)\n412\t\n413\t        Raises:\n414\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n415\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n416\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n417\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n418\t        \"\"\"\n419\t        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n420\t        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n421\t        if cached_result is not None:\n422\t            # Log cache statistics periodically\n423\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n424\t\n425\t            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n426\t            empty_breakdown = {\n427\t                \"cached\": True,\n428\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n429\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n430\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n431\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n432\t                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n433\t                \"process_ms\": 0,\n434\t                \"total_ms\": 0,\n435\t                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n436\t            }\n437\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n438\t\n439\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n440\t        retry_count = 0\n441\t        raw_response = None\n442\t\n443\t        try:\n444\t            # Measure total classification time\n445\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n446\t\n447\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n448\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n449\t\n450\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n451\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n452\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n453\t\n454\t            # Build user message\n455\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n456\t\n457\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n458\t\n459\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n460\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n461\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n462\t\n463\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n464\t\n465\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n466\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n467\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n468\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n469\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n470\t                user_message=user_message,\n471\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n472\t                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n473\t                temperature=0.0,\n474\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n475\t            )\n476\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n477\t\n478\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n479\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n480\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n481\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n482\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n483\t\n484\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n485\t            raw_response = response\n486\t\n487\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n488\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n489\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\n490\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n491\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n492\t\n493\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n494\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n495\t            try:\n496\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n497\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n498\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n499\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n500\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n501\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n502\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n503\t                raise e\n504\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n505\t\n506\t            # Measure classification processing time\n507\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n508\t            classifications = []\n509\t\n510\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n511\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n512\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n513\t                try:\n514\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n515\t                except ValueError:\n516\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n517\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n518\t\n519\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n520\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n521\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n522\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n523\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n524\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n525\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n526\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n527\t                )\n528\t                classifications.append(classification)\n529\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n530\t\n531\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n532\t\n533\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n534\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n535\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n536\t            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n537\t            logger.info(\n538\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n539\t                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n540\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n541\t                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n542\t                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n543\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n544\t                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n545\t                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n546\t                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n547\t            )\n548\t\n549\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n550\t\n551\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n552\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n553\t            timing_breakdown = {\n554\t                \"cached\": False,  # Cache miss - result from fresh ZAI proxy call\n555\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n556\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n557\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n558\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n559\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n560\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n561\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n562\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n563\t            }\n564\t\n565\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n566\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n567\t\n568\t            # Log cache statistics periodically (including this cache miss)\n569\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n570\t\n571\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n572\t\n573\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n574\t            \"\"\"\n575\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n576\t\n577\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n578\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n579\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n580\t            \"\"\"\n581\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n582\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n583\t\n584\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n585\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n586\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n587\t                retry_count += 1\n588\t                try:\n589\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n590\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n591\t                        utterance=utterance,\n592\t                        session_id=session_id,\n593\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n594\t                    )\n595\t                    return classifications, _\n596\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n597\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n598\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n599\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n600\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n601\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n602\t                    ) from retry_e\n603\t\n604\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n605\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n606\t                parse_error=str(e),\n607\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n608\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n609\t            ) from e\n610\t\n611\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n612\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n613\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n614\t\n615\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n616\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n617\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n618\t\n619\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n620\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n621\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n622\t\n623\t        except Exception as e:\n624\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n625\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n626\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n627\t\n628\t    async def route_utterance(\n629\t        self,\n630\t        utterance: str,\n631\t        utterance_id: str,\n632\t        session_id: str,\n633\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n634\t        \"\"\"\n635\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n636\t\n637\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n638\t\n639\t        Args:\n640\t            utterance: The user utterance\n641\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n642\t            session_id: Session ID\n643\t\n644\t        Returns:\n645\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n646\t\n647\t        Raises:\n648\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n649\t        \"\"\"\n650\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n651\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n652\t\n653\t        try:\n654\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n655\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n656\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n657\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n658\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n659\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n660\t\n661\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n662\t            try:\n663\t                store = await self._get_store()\n664\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n665\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n666\t            except Exception as e:\n667\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n668\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n669\t\n670\t            routed_intents = []\n671\t            for classification in classifications:\n672\t                # Create intent ID\n673\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n674\t\n675\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n676\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n677\t                    classification=classification,\n678\t                    session_id=session_id,\n679\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n680\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n681\t                )\n682\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n683\t\n684\t            return routed_intents\n685\t\n686\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n687\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n688\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n689\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n690\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n691\t                utterance=utterance,\n692\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n693\t                session_id=session_id,\n694\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n695\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n696\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n697\t            )\n698\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n699\t\n700\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n701\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n702\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n703\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n704\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n705\t                utterance=utterance,\n706\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n707\t                session_id=session_id,\n708\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n709\t            )\n710\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n711\t\n712\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n713\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n714\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n715\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n716\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n717\t                utterance=utterance,\n718\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n719\t                session_id=session_id,\n720\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n721\t            )\n722\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n723\t\n724\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n725\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n726\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n727\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n728\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n729\t                utterance=utterance,\n730\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n731\t                session_id=session_id,\n732\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n733\t            )\n734\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n735\t\n736\t    async def process_intent(\n737\t        self,\n738\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n739\t    ) -> dict:\n740\t        \"\"\"\n741\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n742\t\n743\t        Args:\n744\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n745\t\n746\t        Returns:\n747\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n748\t        \"\"\"\n749\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n750\t\n751\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n752\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n753\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n754\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n755\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n756\t        # the dispatch itself.\n757\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n758\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n759\t\n760\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n761\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n762\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n763\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n764\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n765\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n766\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n767\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n768\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n769\t            )\n770\t\n771\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n772\t            store = await self._get_store()\n773\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n774\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n775\t                \"resolved\",\n776\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n777\t            )\n778\t\n779\t            return {\n780\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n781\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n782\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n783\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n784\t            }\n785\t\n786\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n787\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n788\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n789\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n790\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n791\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n792\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n793\t            )\n794\t\n795\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n796\t            store = await self._get_store()\n797\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n798\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n799\t                \"resolved\",\n800\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n801\t            )\n802\t\n803\t            return {\n804\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n805\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n806\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n807\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n808\t            }\n809\t\n810\t        try:\n811\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n812\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n813\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n814\t            else:\n815\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n816\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n817\t        except Exception:\n818\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n819\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n820\t            raise\n821\t\n822\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n823\t        return result\n824\t\n825\t    async def _persist_timings(\n826\t        self,\n827\t        intent_id: str,\n828\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n829\t    ) -> None:\n830\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n831\t        try:\n832\t            store = await self._get_store()\n833\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n834\t        except Exception as e:\n835\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n836\t\n837\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n838\t        self,\n839\t )\n892\t\n893\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n894\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n895\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n896\t           # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n920\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n921\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n922\t\n923\t        ))\n935\t\n936\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n937\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n938\t                  })\n960\t\n961\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n962\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n963\t                await getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# --- Intent Cache with TTL ----------------------------------------------------\nclass IntentCache:\n    \"\"\"\n    In-memory cache store for intent classifications with TTL support.\n\n    Storage format: {cache_key -> (intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp)}\n    - cache_key: SHA256 hash of utterance + optional context\n    - intent_mapping: list[IntentClassification] to return on cache hit\n    - expiry_timestamp: Unix timestamp when entry expires (now + ttl_seconds)\n\n    Automatic cleanup:\n    - Expired entries are removed on get() when cache size > 1000\n    - Oldest entry is evicted when cache reaches capacity\n\n    Thread-safety: Not thread-safe - assumes single-threaded async execution.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int = 300, max_size: int = 1000):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the cache.\n\n        Args:\n            ttl_seconds: Time-to-live for cache entries in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes)\n            max_size: Maximum number of entries before eviction (default: 1000)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds\n        self.max_size = max_size\n        self._cache: dict[str, tuple[list, float]] = {}\n        self._cache_hits = 0\n        self._cache_misses = 0\n        self._stats_log_interval = 50  # Log stats every N operations\n\n    def get(self, key: str) -> list | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Retrieve cached intent mapping if exists and not expired.\n\n        Args:\n            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n\n        Returns:\n            Cached intent mapping if exists and not expired, None otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        # Automatic cleanup: if cache size > 1000, remove all expired entries first\n        # This prevents memory leaks from accumulating expired entries\n        if len(self._cache) > 1000:\n            removed = self._cleanup_expired()\n            if removed > 0:\n                logger.debug(f\"Auto-cleanup removed {removed} expired entries (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n        if key in self._cache:\n            intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp = self._cache[key]\n\n            # Check if entry has expired\n            if time.time() < expiry_timestamp:\n                self._cache_hits += 1\n                age = time.time() - (expiry_timestamp - self.ttl_seconds)\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache HIT for key {key[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n                return intent_mapping\n            else:\n                # Remove expired entry\n                del self._cache[key]\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache EXPIRED for key {key[:8]}\")\n\n        # Cache miss or expired\n        self._cache_misses += 1\n        return None\n\n    def set(self, key: str, value: list) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Store intent mapping with expiry timestamp.\n\n        Args:\n            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n            value: Intent mapping to cache (list[IntentClassification])\n        \"\"\"\n        # Prune cache if at capacity (evict oldest entry - first in dict)\n        if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:\n            oldest_key = next(iter(self._cache))\n            del self._cache[oldest_key]\n            logger.debug(f\"Cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n        # Store with expiry timestamp\n        expiry_timestamp = time.time() + self.ttl_seconds\n        self._cache[key] = (value, expiry_timestamp)\n        logger.debug(f\"Cache STORED key {key[:8]} (cache size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n    def _cleanup_expired(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Remove all expired entries from the cache.\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of entries removed\n        \"\"\"\n        current_time = time.time()\n        expired_keys = [\n            key for key, (_, expiry) in self._cache.items()\n            if expiry < current_time\n        ]\n\n        for key in expired_keys:\n            del self._cache[key]\n\n        if expired_keys:\n            logger.debug(f\"Cache cleanup removed {len(expired_keys)} expired entries\")\n\n        return len(expired_keys)\n\n    def get_stats(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get cache statistics.\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with hits, misses, hit_rate, size\n        \"\"\"\n        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n        hit_rate = (self._cache_hits / total_requests * 100) if total_requests > 0 else 0.0\n\n        return {\n            \"hits\": self._cache_hits,\n            \"misses\": self._cache_misses,\n            \"hit_rate\": hit_rate,\n            \"size\": len(self._cache),\n        }\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear the cache and reset statistics.\"\"\"\n        self._cache.clear()\n        self._cache_hits = 0\n        self._cache_misses = 0\n        logger.debug(\"Cache cleared\")\n\n    def should_log_stats(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if stats should be logged based on operation interval.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if stats should be logged, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n        return total_requests > 0 and total_requests % self._stats_log_interval == 0\n\n\n# Router error types for degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call quota exhausted.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n        self.parse_error = parse_error\n        self.raw_output = raw_output\n        self.retry_count = retry_count\n        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n\n    Latency optimizations:\n    - LRU cache for repeated utterances (5-minute TTL)\n    - Reduced max_tokens (128 vs 256) for faster generation\n    - Dedicated 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None, cache_ttl: int = 300):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._router_zai_client = None  # Dedicated client with 10s timeout\n        self._reload_manager = None\n        self._cache = IntentCache(ttl_seconds=cache_ttl)\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_router_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create dedicated ZAI client for router with optimized settings for low latency.\"\"\"\n        if self._router_zai_client is None:\n            from ..escalate.llm import get_router_zai_client\n            # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\n            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\n            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n        return self._router_zai_client\n\n    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\"\"\"\n        import hashlib\n        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\n\n    def generate_cache_key(self, utterance: str, intent_type_context: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate a SHA256 hash key for caching based on utterance and optional context.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance to hash\n            intent_type_context: Optional context string (e.g., intent type, session state)\n\n        Returns:\n            SHA256 hex digest (32 characters) of the combined utterance and context\n\n        Examples:\n            >>> generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", \"status\")\n            'a1b2c3d4e5f6...'  # 32-character hex string\n            >>> generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", None)  # Same as no context\n            'differenthashvalue...'  # Different hash without context\n        \"\"\"\n        import hashlib\n\n        # Handle None/empty context gracefully - treat as empty string\n        context = intent_type_context or \"\"\n\n        # Combine utterance and context with a delimiter to avoid collisions\n        # e.g., \"test\" + \"status\" vs \"teststatus\" + \"\"\n        combined = f\"{utterance}|{context}\"\n\n        # Generate SHA256 hash and return hex digest (32 characters)\n        return hashlib.sha256(combined.encode()).hexdigest()\n\n    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n        return self._cache.get(cache_key)\n\n    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n        self._cache.set(cache_key, classifications)\n\n    def _log_cache_stats_if_needed(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Log cache hit rate statistics periodically.\"\"\"\n        if self._cache.should_log_stats():\n            stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Router cache statistics: \"\n                f\"hits={stats['hits']} misses={stats['misses']} \"\n                f\"hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.1f}% \"\n                f\"total_requests={stats['hits'] + stats['misses']} \"\n                f\"cache_size={stats['size']}\"\n            )\n\n    def _clear_cache(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear the router cache. Used primarily for testing and diagnostics.\"\"\"\n        self._cache.clear()\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n\n        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n\n        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self._load_router_prompt()\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> tuple[list[IntentClassification], dict]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (classifications list, timing_breakdown dict)\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n        if cached_result is not None:\n            # Log cache statistics periodically\n            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n\n            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n            empty_breakdown = {\n                \"cached\": True,\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n                \"process_ms\": 0,\n                \"total_ms\": 0,\n                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n            }\n            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            # Measure total classification time\n            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n\n            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Build user message\n            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n                temperature=0.0,\n                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n            )\n            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n\n            # Extract content and timing from response\n            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n            calculated_inference_ms = None\n            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n\n            # Measure JSON parsing time\n            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n            try:\n                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n            except ParseLLMError as e:\n                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n                raise e\n            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure classification processing time\n            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n\n            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n\n            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            logger.info(\n                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n\n            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n            timing_breakdown = {\n                \"cached\": False,  # Cache miss - result from fresh ZAI proxy call\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n            }\n\n            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n\n            # Log cache statistics periodically (including this cache miss)\n            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n\n            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n\n        except ParseLLMError as e:\n            \"\"\"\n            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n\n            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n            \"\"\"\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                    return classifications, _\n                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n            try:\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n                  timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n           routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n           \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n           fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n    # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n
